https://t.co/h8RWOeOol4
Alright! Our wind particles are now powered by real weather wind speed and direction data... gonna call it a night. Another fun stream! https://t.co/iNkuErpFrd
The cool part is that we're going to use our simulated wind particles to reveal a dot-density layer underneath- it looks fun with the big circles and prototype data. https://t.co/Tsz1w7OLlh
Now I am taking the simulation and animating adding new wind particles to it every few ticks, we'll add a lot more particles and make them smaller later. https://t.co/NEVu8o9Ds6
Mow mow made a brief guest appearance https://t.co/rKNVmqqvAT

Alright, our wind data is now added to our D3 force simulation, I'm gonna take a quick break, when I'm back we're going to refactor so we can continuously add new wind nodes and see them animate. https://t.co/mvsrlCHk33
Got our weather XML parsed, now working on building proper wind particles from the weather data. https://t.co/VRM87896rN
Going live now https://t.co/mvsrlCHk33

Gonna do another livestream later today at 4PM EST. It has  been a really interesting experiment to work with GitHub copilot while creating data visualizations in Vue. I have been pleasantly surprised. Come hang out! https://t.co/mvsrlCHk33 https://t.co/jtZmybMcbw
